assumptionsLockedForExecution: New composer contracts live under apps/flagship-foundry/schemas/ to match the current canonical repo layout.
Declared composer artifacts persist under a composer-specific subtree in apps/flagship-foundry/state/ and stay separate from plan/run state.
Control-plane composer routes use /composer/* plus /catalogs/building-blocks following the existing route naming style.
Cursor Companion remains the canonical write-capable surface; graph-viewer remains read-only.
Composer launch remains feature-flagged and approval-gated by default.

dependencyGroups.milestone: M2 — control plane and operator surfaces

workOrders.title: composer-schema-pack
constraints: Keep compile logic separate from execution logic.
Keep declared artifact management separate from observed runtime/session projection.
Treat repo-native personas, skills, runbooks, templates, and model-routing policy as read-only authorities.
Preserve existing run-control as the sole execution authority.
Do not enable execution-affecting endpoints without explicit approval artifacts and a feature flag.
Do not introduce new dependencies unless the work order explicitly authorizes it.
Place all new contracts under apps/flagship-foundry/schemas to match the current repo layout.
Use JSON Schema draft 2020-12 and keep additionalProperties false for external-facing contracts unless explicitly justified.
Keep contract names aligned with the approved architecture packet terminology.
frameworkRules: Canonical app root is apps/flagship-foundry within the Nx repo root.
packages/core and apps/control-plane use Node ESM modules.
apps/cursor-companion remains CommonJS/VS Code extension entrypoint via extension.js.
apps/graph-viewer uses React 19 + TypeScript + Vite and must continue to build with its local build script.
Verification must use existing node --test and Nx targets before adding any new harness.
testsToCreateFirst: Create tests/composer-schema-contracts.test.mjs before adding schema files.
Start with failing fixtures that prove enum, required-field, and additionalProperties enforcement.
inputSchemas: Approved architecture packet contract names and field responsibilities.
Existing flagship-foundry schema conventions under apps/flagship-foundry/schemas/*.json.
outputSchemas: Validated JSON Schemas for registry index, composed configuration, pipeline definition, compilation result, execution request, and session projection.

functionSpecs.name: loadComposerSchemas
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
for each requested schema path:
  read file as UTF-8
  JSON.parse contents
  store parsed schema by basename
return parsed schema map

functionSpecs.name: validateComposerFixtureSet
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
create JSON Schema validator from provided schema
for each valid fixture: assert validator returns true
for each invalid fixture: assert validator returns false
collect unexpected pass/fail details
return counts and errors

expectedOutputs: Six machine-readable composer/orchestration schemas under apps/flagship-foundry/schemas.
One contract test file that loads each schema and validates at least one good fixture and one bad fixture per schema.
acceptanceCriteria: All six schema files exist and each validates its approved happy-path fixture.
The test suite rejects malformed fixtures for missing required fields, invalid enums, and undeclared extra properties.
Schema naming and field names match the approved architecture packet contract set.
docsRequirements: Add a short contract inventory header comment at the top of tests/composer-schema-contracts.test.mjs describing fixture coverage.
knowledgeCapture: Record any contract field that required interpretation beyond the architecture packet so the verifier can review it explicitly.
approvalRequirements: None; contract generation is pre-execution and feature-flag neutral.
rollbackNotes: Delete only the six new schema files and the companion test file if the contract pack is rejected.

workOrders.title: registry-projection-loader
constraints: Keep compile logic separate from execution logic.
Keep declared artifact management separate from observed runtime/session projection.
Treat repo-native personas, skills, runbooks, templates, and model-routing policy as read-only authorities.
Preserve existing run-control as the sole execution authority.
Do not enable execution-affecting endpoints without explicit approval artifacts and a feature flag.
Do not introduce new dependencies unless the work order explicitly authorizes it.
Registry projection must be read-only over repo-native sources and must not rewrite persona/skill/runbook files.
Projection output must carry sourceUri and immutability markers for every block.
Do not fold runtime session state into the registry index.
frameworkRules: Canonical app root is apps/flagship-foundry within the Nx repo root.
packages/core and apps/control-plane use Node ESM modules.
apps/cursor-companion remains CommonJS/VS Code extension entrypoint via extension.js.
apps/graph-viewer uses React 19 + TypeScript + Vite and must continue to build with its local build script.
Verification must use existing node --test and Nx targets before adding any new harness.
testsToCreateFirst: Create tests/composer-registry.test.mjs before implementing composer-registry.mjs.
Start with a failing test that asserts at least one block exists for each required kind in the projection.
inputSchemas: Repo-native personas/skills/roles/model routes as input authorities.
outputSchemas: Schema-compliant building-block index document and projection helper functions.

functionSpecs.name: buildBuildingBlockIndex
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
scan repo-authority folders and config files that define personas, skills, roles, runbook phases, templates, and model routes
normalize each source into a block record with blockId, kind, displayName, sourceUri, immutability, capabilities, and constraints
sort blocks deterministically by kind then blockId
return the completed BuildingBlockLibraryIndex document

expectedOutputs: A registry loader that projects personas, skills, agent roles, runbook phases, work-order templates, and model routes into BuildingBlockLibraryIndex.
A focused test file that verifies sourceUri, immutability, and capabilities are present on every projected block.
acceptanceCriteria: buildBuildingBlockIndex() returns schema-compliant output for a fixture workspace.
Each projected block includes a blockId, kind, displayName, sourceUri, immutability, and capabilities array.
No registry function mutates repo-authority source files in place.
docsRequirements: Document in test comments which folders or config files are treated as source authorities for the projection.
knowledgeCapture: Capture any missing block kind in a skipped/expected-failure note rather than silently omitting it.
rollbackNotes: Remove composer-registry.mjs and its test file only; do not touch source authorities.

workOrders.title: composition-artifact-store
constraints: Keep compile logic separate from execution logic.
Keep declared artifact management separate from observed runtime/session projection.
Treat repo-native personas, skills, runbooks, templates, and model-routing policy as read-only authorities.
Preserve existing run-control as the sole execution authority.
Do not enable execution-affecting endpoints without explicit approval artifacts and a feature flag.
Do not introduce new dependencies unless the work order explicitly authorizes it.
Persist declared composer artifacts separately from runtime plans and runs.
Use stable refs and deterministic filenames so recompilation is idempotent.
Store provenance metadata and never overwrite source authorities.
frameworkRules: Canonical app root is apps/flagship-foundry within the Nx repo root.
packages/core and apps/control-plane use Node ESM modules.
apps/cursor-companion remains CommonJS/VS Code extension entrypoint via extension.js.
apps/graph-viewer uses React 19 + TypeScript + Vite and must continue to build with its local build script.
Verification must use existing node --test and Nx targets before adding any new harness.
testsToCreateFirst: Create tests/composer-artifacts.test.mjs before implementing the storage module.
Start with failing tests for create/load/list round-trips and a missing-document error path.
outputSchemas: Stable storage/retrieval helpers for declared composer artifacts.

functionSpecs.name: saveComposedConfiguration
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
validate incoming configuration against schema
derive state path under state/composer/configurations/
write detail document and a lightweight header projection
return configuration ref and timestamp

functionSpecs.name: savePipelineDefinition
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
validate pipeline against schema
derive state path under state/composer/pipelines/
write detail document and header projection
return pipeline ref and timestamp

expectedOutputs: Storage helpers for save/load/list of composed agent configurations and orchestration pipeline definitions.
Tests covering create, update, load, list, and not-found behaviors for declared composer artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Configurations and pipelines persist under a composer-specific state subtree instead of the live run directories.
load* functions return exact saved documents without mutating field order or dropping provenance metadata.
list* functions return stable headers sorted by updatedAt descending then id.
docsRequirements: Add a top-of-file path contract comment in composer-artifacts.mjs documenting the state subtree used.
knowledgeCapture: If state layout needs a second header/index optimization, note it as a follow-up rather than adding speculative indexing now.
rollbackNotes: Delete state/composer/* documents created by tests and remove composer-artifacts.mjs if the storage layout is rejected.

workOrders.title: pipeline-compiler-core
constraints: Keep compile logic separate from execution logic.
Keep declared artifact management separate from observed runtime/session projection.
Treat repo-native personas, skills, runbooks, templates, and model-routing policy as read-only authorities.
Preserve existing run-control as the sole execution authority.
Do not enable execution-affecting endpoints without explicit approval artifacts and a feature flag.
Do not introduce new dependencies unless the work order explicitly authorizes it.
Compilation must emit draft work-order refs or a draft plan ref; it may not launch runs directly.
Compilation must be deterministic for identical pipeline inputs.
Warnings and errors must be surfaced in PipelineCompilationResult instead of hidden in logs.
frameworkRules: Canonical app root is apps/flagship-foundry within the Nx repo root.
packages/core and apps/control-plane use Node ESM modules.
apps/cursor-companion remains CommonJS/VS Code extension entrypoint via extension.js.
apps/graph-viewer uses React 19 + TypeScript + Vite and must continue to build with its local build script.
Verification must use existing node --test and Nx targets before adding any new harness.
testsToCreateFirst: Create tests/pipeline-compiler.test.mjs before implementing the compiler.
Start with a failing cycle-detection test and a happy-path linear compile test.
outputSchemas: Deterministic pipeline compiler producing PipelineCompilationResult documents.

functionSpecs.name: compilePipelineToDraftArtifacts
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
validate pipeline schema
topologically sort stages and detect cycles or missing refs
derive draft work-order metadata from lifecycleStage, configurationRef, and governance gates
accumulate required approvals and warnings
return PipelineCompilationResult with draft refs, approvals, warnings, and errors

expectedOutputs: A compiler that turns an orchestration pipeline definition into a draft-work-order-oriented compilation result.
Tests that cover linear pipelines, fan-out, missing dependency references, and approval-gated stages.
acceptanceCriteria: compilePipelineToDraftArtifacts() returns PipelineCompilationResult for valid pipelines and surfaces errors for invalid dependencies.
Compilation preserves stage order and respects declared dependsOn relationships.
Compilation never calls the existing run-control functions directly.
docsRequirements: Describe the compiler invariants in a header comment inside pipeline-compiler.mjs: deterministic order, no execution, explicit warnings/errors.
knowledgeCapture: Record any stage-to-work-order field mapping that is not obvious from the schemas so the verifier can review it.
rollbackNotes: Remove pipeline-compiler.mjs and its tests if draft-artifact compilation is redesigned.

workOrders.title: execution-policy-gateway-core
constraints: Keep compile logic separate from execution logic.
Keep declared artifact management separate from observed runtime/session projection.
Treat repo-native personas, skills, runbooks, templates, and model-routing policy as read-only authorities.
Preserve existing run-control as the sole execution authority.
Do not enable execution-affecting endpoints without explicit approval artifacts and a feature flag.
Do not introduce new dependencies unless the work order explicitly authorizes it.
Existing run-control remains the sole execution authority.
Launch, resume, stop, and rerun-failed-slice require approval artifacts or an already-approved plan/work-order.
Feature flag default must remain off until human approval changes it.
frameworkRules: Canonical app root is apps/flagship-foundry within the Nx repo root.
packages/core and apps/control-plane use Node ESM modules.
apps/cursor-companion remains CommonJS/VS Code extension entrypoint via extension.js.
apps/graph-viewer uses React 19 + TypeScript + Vite and must continue to build with its local build script.
Verification must use existing node --test and Nx targets before adding any new harness.
testsToCreateFirst: Create tests/execution-policy-gateway.test.mjs before implementing the gateway module.
Start with failing tests for approval enforcement and feature-flag-off behavior.
outputSchemas: Execution policy gateway module with explicit approve-or-deny outcomes and delegation to existing run-control only.

functionSpecs.name: validateExecutionRequest
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
validate request schema and required action/sourceType fields
check approval refs against action and sourceType rules
check runtimeMode against allowedRuntimeModes from the approved source
return normalized request or validation errors

functionSpecs.name: dispatchExecutionRequest
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
call validateExecutionRequest
if composer execution feature flag is off: reject with policy error
map allowed action to existing run-control helper
return normalized DashboardExecutionSession projection referencing the underlying run

expectedOutputs: A gateway module that validates execution requests, checks approvals/runtime mode legality, and delegates only approved actions to existing run-control.
Tests covering denied launch without approval, denied illegal runtime mode, approved delegation, and feature-flag disable paths.
acceptanceCriteria: validateExecutionRequest() rejects missing approval refs for execution-affecting actions.
dispatchExecutionRequest() delegates only to existing run-control helpers and records the resulting session ref.
A disabled feature flag blocks composer launch paths without affecting legacy run-control behavior.
docsRequirements: Document every policy denial reason string in tests so the UI layer can map them predictably.
knowledgeCapture: Any ambiguity around runtimeMode legality must be surfaced as an explicit TODO for verifier review instead of auto-permitting.
approvalRequirements: Human approval required before any execution-affecting endpoint using this gateway is enabled by default.
rollbackNotes: Remove execution-policy-gateway.mjs and revert only the new gateway tests; leave existing run-control untouched.

constraints: Keep compile logic separate from execution logic.
Keep declared artifact management separate from observed runtime/session projection.
Treat repo-native personas, skills, runbooks, templates, and model-routing policy as read-only authorities.
Preserve existing run-control as the sole execution authority.
Do not enable execution-affecting endpoints without explicit approval artifacts and a feature flag.
Do not introduce new dependencies unless the work order explicitly authorizes it.
Follow existing control-plane route style and critical payload validation behavior.
Expose composer CRUD under a dedicated namespace and keep route semantics aligned with existing /plans and /catalogs conventions.
Do not mix compile or launch behavior into this CRUD slice.
frameworkRules: Canonical app root is apps/flagship-foundry within the Nx repo root.
packages/core and apps/control-plane use Node ESM modules.
apps/cursor-companion remains CommonJS/VS Code extension entrypoint via extension.js.

Start with failing tests for GET /catalogs/building-blocks and POST/GET /composer/configurations.
outputSchemas: Composer CRUD routes in the control plane.

functionSpecs.name: handleComposerCrudRoutes
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
if GET /catalogs/building-blocks: return buildBuildingBlockIndex()
if POST /composer/configurations: validate and save composed configuration
if GET configuration routes: list or load configuration
if POST /composer/pipelines and GET pipeline routes: save/list/load pipeline definitions
otherwise return no match

expectedOutputs: Control-plane routes for listing building blocks and save/load/list of composed configurations and pipelines.
Route tests covering happy-path CRUD and 404/not-found behaviors.
acceptanceCriteria: The control plane exposes read-only building-block listing and declared composer artifact CRUD without any execution side effects.
Route handlers return JSON with stable refs and timestamps from the composer artifact store.
Invalid payloads receive 400 responses with explicit error messages.
docsRequirements: Update route-level inline comments in server.mjs only where they clarify new composer namespaces; do not rewrite unrelated route comments.
knowledgeCapture: Note any route naming divergence from existing control-plane conventions for verifier review.
rollbackNotes: Revert only the new /catalogs/building-blocks and /composer/* CRUD branches in server.mjs plus their tests.

workOrders.title: composer-control-plane-compile-launch-routes
constraints: Keep compile logic separate from execution logic.
Keep declared artifact management separate from observed runtime/session projection.
Treat repo-native personas, skills, runbooks, templates, and model-routing policy as read-only authorities.
Preserve existing run-control as the sole execution authority.
Do not enable execution-affecting endpoints without explicit approval artifacts and a feature flag.
Do not introduce new dependencies unless the work order explicitly authorizes it.
Compile and launch routes must stay separate from CRUD routes in both code branches and tests.
Launch routes must invoke the execution-policy gateway rather than existing run-control directly.
The compile route may create draft refs but may not start execution.
frameworkRules: Canonical app root is apps/flagship-foundry within the Nx repo root.
packages/core and apps/control-plane use Node ESM modules.
apps/cursor-companion remains CommonJS/VS Code extension entrypoint via extension.js.
apps/graph-viewer uses React 19 + TypeScript + Vite and must continue to build with its local build script.

testsToCreateFirst: Create tests/composer-control-plane-compile-launch.test.mjs before editing server.mjs for compile/launch routes.
Start with a failing compile-only test that proves no run is created as a side effect.
outputSchemas: Composer compile/launch/session routes in the control plane.

functionSpecs.name: handleComposerExecutionRoutes
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
if POST /composer/compile: load pipeline and call compilePipelineToDraftArtifacts
if POST /composer/execute: validate request through execution-policy gateway and return session projection
if POST /composer/executions/:id/{pause|resume|stop}: route action through execution-policy gateway
if GET execution routes: return session projection payload

expectedOutputs: Control-plane routes for compile, request-approval-ready launch, session load/list, and pause/resume/stop via the gateway.
Tests covering compile-only behavior, denied launch without approval, approved launch, and session projection retrieval.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /composer/compile returns PipelineCompilationResult and does not start a run.
POST /composer/execute and /composer/executions/:id/{pause|resume|stop} go through the execution-policy gateway.
GET /composer/executions and /composer/executions/:id return session projection payloads suitable for UI polling.
docsRequirements: Record the compile-vs-launch separation in the test file header so future edits do not collapse the boundary.
knowledgeCapture: Capture any response-shape assumption needed by the UI layers in the test file, not in hidden implementation notes.
approvalRequirements: Human approval required before enabling /composer/execute routes by default.
Feature flag must remain default-off until verifier sign-off.
rollbackNotes: Remove only compile/execute/session branches and tests; keep CRUD routes intact.

functionSpecs.name: createComposerOverlayModel
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
group registry blocks by kind for browsing
attach saved configurations and pipelines to overlay sections
if compile result exists: surface warnings, required approvals, and generated refs
if session exists: surface current state, approvals, evidence ref, and logs ref
return render-ready overlay model

expectedOutputs: Companion helper modules for composer API calls and overlay HTML/state rendering.
An updated extension command path that opens a composer overlay with browse, compose, compile, approve-ready execute, and refresh controls.
A focused Node test file covering helper-module state transitions and payload formatting.
acceptanceCriteria: The extension can render a composer overlay without requiring a second editor surface.
The overlay can list building blocks, list/save/load configurations, list/save/load pipelines, call compile, and call execute against approved sources.
All control-plane requests flow through composer-client.js rather than hand-built fetch calls sprinkled throughout extension.js.
docsRequirements: Update apps/cursor-companion/README.md with the composer overlay command flow and control-plane prerequisites.
knowledgeCapture: Document any UI copy needed to explain approval-required states before execution.
approvalRequirements: Execution buttons must remain visibly gated when compile results list required approvals.
rollbackNotes: Revert only composer-client.js, composer-panel.js, extension.js overlay wiring, README updates, and the test file.

functionSpecs.name: buildPipelineGraphProjection
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
emit one graph node per pipeline stage
emit one edge per dependsOn relationship
mark nodes and edges that cross approval boundaries
attach blastRadiusRef and requiredApprovals overlay data when available
return a deterministic view model for the graph viewer

expectedOutputs: A pure projection helper that turns a pipeline definition into node/edge/view-model data for graph rendering.
Tests covering linear, fan-out, and approval-gated stages.
acceptanceCriteria: Projection output contains deterministic node ids, edge ids, stage labels, and approval boundary markers.
A pipeline with approval gates yields overlay markers without mutating the input pipeline definition.
Projection can attach blastRadiusRef when present in compilation outputs.
docsRequirements: Add a short comment in the test file describing why this projection must stay read-only.
knowledgeCapture: Any extra overlay field added for visualization must be documented in the test fixtures.
rollbackNotes: Remove only the projection helper and its tests.

functionSpecs.name: renderPipelineTopologyView
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
receive precomputed projection model
render nodes and edges using existing graph-viewer primitives
show approval overlays and blast-radius badges in a read-only panel
render fallback empty state when no pipeline is selected

functionSpecs.name: renderSessionProjectionView
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
render session state, timeline summary, approval refs, verifier status, evidence ref, and logs ref
surface read-only badges and links/pointers only
avoid any start/pause/resume/stop controls in this projection surface

A read-only session/evidence view that displays session state, verifier status, evidence target, and logs pointer.
acceptanceCriteria: The graph viewer can render pipeline topology and session projection data without adding a second editor surface.
App.tsx routes cleanly between existing views and the new composer views.
The graph-viewer build passes after the new composer views are added.

functionSpecs.name: createSessionPanelModel

derive human-readable status badge from session.state and verifierStatus
map timeline entries into compact rows
group continuity/evidence/log refs into render rows
surface warnings for missing approvals or evidence refs
return render-ready panel model

expectedOutputs: A companion session panel for polling/rendering composer session state in the write-capable surface.
Updated graph-viewer read-only session/evidence panels fed by shared session projection data.
A focused companion helper test covering session badge rendering and stale-session refresh decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: Cursor Companion can display session state, approvals, verifier status, continuity ref, evidence target ref, and logs pointer for composer runs.
Graph viewer displays the same session projection fields in read-only form without action buttons.
Both surfaces degrade gracefully when session polling returns partial data.
docsRequirements: Update session copy in the companion and graph-viewer only where it clarifies declared-vs-observed truth.
knowledgeCapture: Document any polling interval or stale-session decision made for the companion session panel.
rollbackNotes: Revert session-panel.js, related extension wiring, graph-viewer session panel updates, and the companion helper test only.

workOrders.title: verification-and-hardening
constraints: Keep compile logic separate from execution logic.
Keep declared artifact management separate from observed runtime/session projection.
Treat repo-native personas, skills, runbooks, templates, and model-routing policy as read-only authorities.
Preserve existing run-control as the sole execution authority.
Do not enable execution-affecting endpoints without explicit approval artifacts and a feature flag.
Do not introduce new dependencies unless the work order explicitly authorizes it.
Verification must be independent enough to disagree with builder claims.
Security review must cover approval checks, trust-boundary enforcement, and feature-flag default-off behavior.
Performance review must focus on graph-viewer build stability and session polling refresh behavior, not speculative optimization.
frameworkRules: Canonical app root is apps/flagship-foundry within the Nx repo root.
packages/core and apps/control-plane use Node ESM modules.
apps/cursor-companion remains CommonJS/VS Code extension entrypoint via extension.js.
apps/graph-viewer uses React 19 + TypeScript + Vite and must continue to build with its local build script.
Verification must use existing node --test and Nx targets before adding any new harness.
testsToCreateFirst: Create composer-integration.test.mjs and composer-feature-flag-regression.test.mjs before writing the hardening checklist.
Start with failing tests for disabled-feature fallback and missing-approval launch denial.
inputSchemas: All composer schemas and shared session/compile contracts.
outputSchemas: Independent verification suite and hardening checklist for the composer slice.

functionSpecs.name: verifyComposerFlow
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
exercise compile route and assert no run side effect
exercise approved execute path and assert session projection is returned
poll session read endpoint and verify observability fields exist
record any security/performance findings in findings array

expectedOutputs: Integration and regression tests covering compose -> compile -> approved execute -> monitor flows.
A hardening checklist documenting security, performance, and feature-flag gates for the composer slice.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration tests prove compile does not execute and approved execute does.
Regression tests prove legacy run-control still works when composer features are disabled.
Hardening checklist explicitly records approval gate, trust-boundary, and graph-viewer build checks.
docsRequirements: Populate apps/flagship-foundry/docs/verification/composer-hardening-checklist.md with explicit PASS/FAIL/TODO sections rather than prose only.
knowledgeCapture: Record any unresolved security or performance finding as a blocker for the release work order.
approvalRequirements: Release work may not proceed until this work order records a PASS-equivalent verdict for approval and feature-flag checks.
rollbackNotes: Remove only new verification artifacts; do not relax any hardening checks to force a pass.

workOrders.title: release-handoff-and-import-packet
constraints: Keep compile logic separate from execution logic.
Keep declared artifact management separate from observed runtime/session projection.
Treat repo-native personas, skills, runbooks, templates, and model-routing policy as read-only authorities.
Preserve existing run-control as the sole execution authority.
Do not enable execution-affecting endpoints without explicit approval artifacts and a feature flag.
Do not introduce new dependencies unless the work order explicitly authorizes it.
Release documentation must consume verification evidence and not restate builder claims as facts.
Rollout and rollback steps must preserve default-off feature-flag posture until approval is granted.
Import guidance must reference the ready-now work-order artifacts and verification outputs explicitly.
frameworkRules: Canonical app root is apps/flagship-foundry within the Nx repo root.
packages/core and apps/control-plane use Node ESM modules.
apps/cursor-companion remains CommonJS/VS Code extension entrypoint via extension.js.
apps/graph-viewer uses React 19 + TypeScript + Vite and must continue to build with its local build script.
Verification must use existing node --test and Nx targets before adding any new harness.
testsToCreateFirst: Tests to create first: none in this slice; this is evidence-backed documentation work and verifies by artifact presence and content review.
outputSchemas: Release/handoff/import documentation for the composer slice.

functionSpecs.name: buildComposerReleasePacket
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
read verification checklist and summarize only PASS/FAIL-backed evidence
write rollout/rollback instructions with feature-flag-off default preserved
write handoff doc with evidence references and next-step pointers
write import packet for the next workspace
return created file paths

expectedOutputs: Release-ready rollout/rollback doc, handoff doc, and import packet for the next workspace.
acceptanceCriteria: Release docs reference verification evidence rather than assumptions.
Rollback steps explicitly disable composer feature flags and preserve legacy run-control.
Import packet tells the next workspace to consume uploaded artifacts directly instead of reconstructing from memory.
docsRequirements: All three docs must include explicit evidence references and next-step actions.
knowledgeCapture: If rollout guidance uncovers missing evidence, push that note back to WO-007A instead of guessing.
approvalRequirements: Human approval is required before any rollout step changes the composer feature flag from off to on.
rollbackNotes: Delete only the release/handoff/import docs if release sequencing changes.

workOrders.title: learning-capture-and-kb-promotion
constraints: Keep compile logic separate from execution logic.
Keep declared artifact management separate from observed runtime/session projection.
Treat repo-native personas, skills, runbooks, templates, and model-routing policy as read-only authorities.
Preserve existing run-control as the sole execution authority.
Do not enable execution-affecting endpoints without explicit approval artifacts and a feature flag.
Do not introduce new dependencies unless the work order explicitly authorizes it.
Promote only verified learning; do not write unverified implementation folklore into knowledge assets.
Separate observations from recommendations in the learning record.
KB promotion candidates must be clearly marked candidate/deferred/approved.
frameworkRules: Canonical app root is apps/flagship-foundry within the Nx repo root.
packages/core and apps/control-plane use Node ESM modules.
apps/cursor-companion remains CommonJS/VS Code extension entrypoint via extension.js.
apps/graph-viewer uses React 19 + TypeScript + Vite and must continue to build with its local build script.
Verification must use existing node --test and Nx targets before adding any new harness.
testsToCreateFirst: Tests to create first: none in this slice; this is verified-learning capture and verifies by schema-aligned content review.
outputSchemas: Learning artifacts for future KB or doctrine promotion.

functionSpecs.name: buildComposerLearningRecord
functionSpecs.pseudocode:
collect only verified findings from verification and release artifacts
separate context, observation, recommendation, and applicable scope
mark promotion status candidate/approved/deferred explicitly
write learning record and any KB promotion candidate notes

expectedOutputs: A learning record summarizing verified lessons from the composer implementation and verification cycle.
A promotion-candidates note for any KB/runbook/doctrine updates that deserve future import.
acceptanceCriteria: The learning record distinguishes context, observation, recommendation, and applicable scope.
Only verified findings from WO-007A and WO-008A are promoted as learning candidates.
Deferred or blocked knowledge items remain explicitly marked instead of promoted silently.
docsRequirements: Use the LearningRecord schema headings directly in composer-learning-record.md for easy future promotion.
knowledgeCapture: This work order is the knowledge-capture step; mark every candidate with approved/deferred status.
approvalRequirements: Only verified learning may be marked approved for KB promotion.
rollbackNotes: Delete only the learning docs if the learning record is superseded.
